headline: Suhakam funding frozen at 2019 levels despite expanded mandate
context: Malaysia's national human rights commission Suhakam received the same RM12 million budget in 2025 as in 2019 despite additional mandated responsibilities.

cards.big: Suhakam budget frozen at RM12 million since 2019 despite 40% mandate expansion
cards.sub: Inadequate funding undermines Malaysia's national human rights institution

cards.big: Human Rights Commission of Malaysia Act 1999 establishes Suhakam's mandate
cards.sub: Paris Principles require adequate funding for national human rights institutions

cards.big: Suhakam downgraded from A to B status by GANHRI in 2022
cards.sub: Funding constraints and appointment process cited as key concerns

cards.big: Suhakam received 3,400 complaints in 2024 but resolved only 890
cards.sub: Case backlog grows annually due to staffing shortages from frozen budget

cards.big: Starving the human rights watchdog of funds is a quiet way to silence it
Commissioners publicly requested RM20 million minimum in Budget 2025 submission
